#  Quantum Computing by Fujitsu 
[ ](https://global.fujitsu/en-global/technology/research/quantum/quantum-computing-architecture)
  * [ The future of Quantum computing ](https://global.fujitsu/en-global/technology/research/quantum#anc-01)
  * [ Fujitsu Quantum technology ](https://global.fujitsu/en-global/technology/research/quantum#anc-02)
  * [ Quantum application development ](https://global.fujitsu/en-global/technology/research/quantum#anc-03)
  * [ Quantum computing knowledge ](https://global.fujitsu/en-global/technology/research/quantum#anc-04)
  * [ Publications ](https://global.fujitsu/en-global/technology/research/quantum/publications)


[ Japanese ](https://global.fujitsu/ja-jp/technology/research/quantum)
## News
  * June 25, 2026
[ Fujitsu will exhibit at Quantum.Tech World 2026 and deliver a presentation. ](https://www.alphaevents.com/events-quantumtechus/speakers/saif-rayyan)
  * June 23, 2026
[ Fujitsu will exhibit at ISC 26 and deliver a presentation. ](https://isc-hpc.com/)

[ Fujitsu will exhibit at Nexus Luxembourg 2026 and deliver a presentation. ](https://nexusluxembourg.com/)


[ More ](https://global.fujitsu/en-global/technology/research/quantum/topics)
## Leading the business with quantum computers in 2030
Quantum computing is a pivotal technology poised to drive significant advancements and transformative changes across society and business. By 2030, as its computing power scales dramatically, Fujitsu believes that a significant disparity will emerge between enterprises leveraging quantum technology and everyone else. Fujitsu is committed to supporting customers in becoming early adopters of quantum computing to maximize the competitive benefits of this transformative era.
[ Press release ](https://global.fujitsu/en-global/pr/news/2025/08/01-01-en)
![](https://global.fujitsu/-/media/Project/Fujitsu/Fujitsu-HQ/technology/research/quantum/fig01-en.png?h=834&iar=0&w=1950&rev=6eb4adcc01264223aaa1dc419c2ce7aa&hash=4CB58910026C6F0364C92C24B1926E7E)
**Solving complex social challenges with quantum technology**
Quantum computers are well suited to rapidly process complex problems that push the limits of conventional computing. This unique ability to perform computations previously deemed intractable will accelerate the creation of new value, and enable groundbreaking discoveries and problem-solving across numerous fields, including materials science, drug discovery, and financial forecasting.
Fujitsu's quantum computing R & D strategy
Fujitsu, in collaboration with leading research institutes worldwide, promotes R & D across all areas of quantum technology - from hardware (such as quantum devices, integration, and quantum state control) to foundational software (quantum platform and quantum software), as well as practical quantum applications, with the goal of progressing towards widespread commercial use of quantum computers.
![Figure showing the overall landscape of collaborative research areas in Fujitsu's quantum computer R&D](https://global.fujitsu/-/media/Project/Fujitsu/Fujitsu-HQ/technology/research/quantum/fig02-en.png?h=1709&iar=0&w=4000&rev=ea45e0cfa5aa4c9190feeeb7d1f2c0b4&hash=1CE753108023769304B68F06D1C84377)
## World-leading quantum computing technology
Fujitsu is dedicated to leading global R&D in quantum technology, and accelerating its real-world adoption.
![](https://global.fujitsu/-/media/Project/Fujitsu/Fujitsu-HQ/technology/research/quantum/tech-img-01-936x578.jpg?h=578&iar=0&w=936&rev=5824dee3f9854a4995db792d411230e5&hash=9846B366F30C6DD14101C3E3E15B028B)
**World-leading 256-qubit superconducting quantum computer**
In April 2025, Fujitsu, in joint development with RIKEN, developed a world-leading 256-qubit superconducting quantum computer. It has been available to companies and research institutions since the first quarter of fiscal 2025.
[ Press release ](https://info.archives.global.fujitsu/global/about/resources/news/press-releases/2025/0422-01.html)
[ Inside Superconducting Quantum Computer. ](https://global.fujitsu/-/media/Project/Fujitsu/Fujitsu-HQ/technology/research/quantum/Inside_Superconducting_Quantum_Computer_en.pdf?rev=3113c8835303489999b119f2276f53e1&hash=7EFFEA3121D6CAECECB629789C84BBDE)
![](https://global.fujitsu/-/media/Project/Fujitsu/Fujitsu-HQ/technology/research/quantum/tech-img-02-936x578.png?h=578&iar=0&w=936&rev=9ec5a8bae6914a7ab19984d03580c625&hash=CACEC799DA0E6899FA9670ED2DF16CEF)
**1,000 qubits computer scheduled for 2026 installation**
Fujitsu is currently developing a 1,000-qubit superconducting quantum computer, scheduled to be installed in a new building at the Fujitsu Technology Park and launched in 2026.
[ Press release ](https://info.archives.global.fujitsu/jp/news/2025/06/en/02-01.pdf)
![](https://global.fujitsu/-/media/Project/Fujitsu/Fujitsu-HQ/technology/research/quantum/tech-img-03-936x578.jpg?h=578&iar=0&w=936&rev=b63e6fd3731b467895106845f4832c9c&hash=E7CE5551B6B6498238FD4066390F22C2)
**National project for the industrialization of quantum computers**
Fujitsu’s medium-term goal is to complete the construction of a superconducting quantum computer with a capacity exceeding 10,000 qubits by fiscal 2030. As part of these efforts, we are participating in a national project promoted by NEDO, which runs until fiscal year 2027.
[ Press release ](https://global.fujitsu/en-global/pr/news/2025/08/01-01-en)
![](https://global.fujitsu/-/media/Project/Fujitsu/Fujitsu-HQ/technology/research/quantum/tech-img-04-936x578.jpg?h=578&iar=0&w=936&rev=772c98a7b91b4ba6b9dd062865d1c445&hash=D2C75661DD3BA4AFEE816C7B116B5C86)
**Diamond spin quantum computer**
Fujitsu is also exploring optional alternatives to superconducting quantum systems. Comparatively, the Diamond spin approach offers superior scalability through optical interconnects. In March 2025, we achieved the world's first high-precision quantum gate operation with an error probability below 0.1%, a crucial step toward large-scale quantum computing.
[ Press release ](https://info.archives.global.fujitsu/global/about/resources/news/press-releases/2025/0324-02.html)
[ Technology news ](https://global.fujitsu/en-global/technology/key-technologies/news/ta-fujitsus-diamond-spin-based-quantum-computer-20240909)
[ TECH BLOG ](https://blog-en.fltech.dev/entry/2025/03/18/highly-accurate-diamond-spin-quantum-computer-en)
![An image of an abstract representation of light streaks moving at high speed through space.](https://global.fujitsu/-/media/Project/Fujitsu/Fujitsu-HQ/technology/research/quantum/quantum-computing-architecture/kv-quantum-computing-architecture-936x578.jpg?h=578&iar=0&w=936&rev=adaf90ed422b4a75b1da33b8dd06e49c&hash=5648D1119041D2923826BE71497D1E64)
**A quantum computing architecture to accelerate the early practical application of quantum computers**
The new STAR Architecture, version 3, brings the practical calculation of complex molecular systems much closer to reality.  
For calculations involving ruthenium (Ru) catalysts, combining it with molecular optimization dramatically reduces the number of required qubits from approximately 2 million with conventional FTQC to about 20,000, and shortens the estimated computation time from around 5,000 days to roughly 5 days.
[ Dedicated page ](https://global.fujitsu/en-global/technology/research/quantum/quantum-computing-architecture)
![](https://global.fujitsu/-/media/Project/Fujitsu/Fujitsu-HQ/technology/research/quantum/tech-img-06-936x578.jpg?h=578&iar=0&w=936&rev=e03a990b77f5435da81a2a3f350c26de&hash=97F1760BBF700D6159D28998D6BFAE4A)
**World's largest 40 qubit quantum computer simulator**
Since October 2023, Fujitsu has made available, the world's largest 40-qubit quantum computer simulator. This is a significant offering to promote the research and development of practical quantum applications across industries.
[ Press release ](https://info.archives.global.fujitsu/global/about/resources/news/press-releases/2023/1005-01.html)
![](https://global.fujitsu/-/media/Project/Fujitsu/Fujitsu-HQ/technology/research/quantum/tech-img-07-936x578.jpg?h=578&iar=0&w=936&rev=ea8cb9d1cc8946a480988dc9fbb72248&hash=D5BD048545FE49B77A41C705FED9F07A)
**Cloud enabled hybrid quantum platform**
Fujitsu provides one of the world's largest combined platforms of quantum computers and quantum simulators. With linkages to external libraries, this allows for seamless switching between noisy real machines and noise-free quantum simulators.
[ Press release ](https://info.archives.global.fujitsu/global/about/resources/news/press-releases/2023/1005-01.html)
![](https://global.fujitsu/-/media/Project/Fujitsu/Fujitsu-HQ/technology/research/quantum/tech-img-08-936x578.jpg?h=578&iar=0&w=936&rev=f355e297036b4ee7936a15c6897616ae&hash=01C33EF34D2008781AC52DD488492BF0)
**Open-source software for quantum cloud services**
Fujitsu has launched an open-source software initiative, one of the largest of its kind globally, for operating quantum computers in the cloud. It covers everything from setup to operation, and users can customize it freely to meet their individual needs.
[ Press release ](https://info.archives.global.fujitsu/global/about/resources/news/press-releases/2025/0324-01.html)
[ TECH BLOG ](https://blog-en.fltech.dev/entry/2025/03/24/oqtopus-release-en)
### Deployments: a solid step toward practical use
[ [Press Release] Fujitsu to introduce superconducting quantum computer system at National Institute of Advanced Industrial Science and Technology ](https://info.archives.global.fujitsu/global/about/resources/news/press-releases/2024/0618-01.html)
Please feel free to contact us for more information or for consultation regarding quantum computer implementation, the use of quantum simulators, and joint research. Fujitsu provides comprehensive support for the use of these quantum computers.
## Empowering enterprise quantum application development
Fujitsu believes that organizations that will lead the upcoming competitive race for quantum computing will be those who have spent sufficient time researching and developing quantum applications to solve intractable challenges in their respective domains. For this purpose, it is imperative to use quantum simulators in conjunction with quantum computers. Fujitsu offers technologies and solutions that integrate its world-leading superconducting quantum computer, one of the world's largest quantum simulators, as well as proprietary AI technologies through a common platform. This empowers customers to rapidly advance their quantum application development, addressing their specific computational requirements, and solving their domain-specific challenges. Joint research is already underway in Japan, with leading companies across various industries, including materials, medical care, and finance.
[ Announcing the Fujitsu $100,000 Quantum Simulator Challenge 2025-26](https://global.fujitsu/en-global/technology/research/article/topics/202512-quantum-simulator-challenge)
![](https://global.fujitsu/-/media/Project/Fujitsu/Fujitsu-HQ/technology/research/article/topics/202512-quantum-simulator-challenge/kv-202512-quantum-simulator-challenge-809x500.jpg?rev=c9b2e577df2847f8b238f2bd76f1839d&hash=1DF9396FD7DECC036F5CE16EFC2B07DA)
[ What is "quantum application development" and why should companies start working on it right now?](https://global.fujitsu/en-global/technology/key-technologies/news/ta-quantum-app-dev-for-enterprises-20250423)
![](https://global.fujitsu/-/media/Project/Fujitsu/Fujitsu-HQ/technology/key-technologies/news/ta-quantum-app-dev-for-enterprises-20250423/kv-quantum-app-dev-for-enterprises-20250423-936x578.jpg?rev=689f225a61be45b99e4450b16a0e5132&hash=C433FB06E3C622B25F963AEE49BD7442)
Solutions from Fujitsu  
Fujitsu offers the following solutions:
  * [ Fujitsu Hybrid Quantum Computing Platform ](https://global.fujitsu/-/media/Project/Fujitsu/Fujitsu-HQ/technology/research/quantum/fujitsu_hybrid_quantum_computing_platform_en.pdf?rev=e9ec2fd765494456bfd0c7d13bea851d&hash=DC42784FA2FDD1FAE9CD34B83B3AFC81)
  * [ OSS Quantum Cloud Platform Open Quantum Toolchain for Operators & Users ](https://github.com/oqtopus-team/oqtopus-cloud)


Fujitsu's quantum computer development roadmap
Fujitsu’s ambitious target for fiscal 2030 involves the construction of a large-scale superconducting quantum computer with a capacity exceeding 10,000 qubits. Furthermore, from fiscal 2030 and beyond, we plan to pursue advanced research initiatives targeting the integration of superconducting and diamond spin-based qubits. By fiscal 2035, we aim to realize a 1,000 logical qubit machine, considering the possibility of multiple interconnected quantum bit-chips. Fujitsu is committed to driving forward the long-term development of practical and industrialized quantum computing solutions across all aspects.
![](https://global.fujitsu/-/media/Project/Fujitsu/Fujitsu-HQ/technology/research/quantum/fig-03-en.png?h=540&iar=0&w=960&rev=f21e08597e414d419106da9610c2f2a2&hash=0A5FB1BAC28EB225C20685280FB65FEB)

Quantum Computing by Fujitsu

A quantum computing architecture to accelerate the early practical application of quantum computers
  • The future of Quantum computing
  • Fujitsu Quantum technology
  • Quantum application development
  • Quantum computing knowledge
  • Publications
Japanese

News

  • June 25, 2026
    Fujitsu will exhibit at Quantum.Tech World 2026 and deliver a presentation.
  • June 23, 2026
    Fujitsu will exhibit at ISC 26 and deliver a presentation.
  • June 10, 2026
    Fujitsu will exhibit at Nexus Luxembourg 2026 and deliver a presentation.
More

Leading the business with quantum computers in 2030

Quantum computing is a pivotal technology poised to drive significant advancements and transformative changes across society and business. By 2030, as its computing power scales dramatically, Fujitsu believes that a significant disparity will emerge between enterprises leveraging quantum technology and everyone else. Fujitsu is committed to supporting customers in becoming early adopters of quantum computing to maximize the competitive benefits of this transformative era.

Press release

Solving complex social challenges with quantum technology

Quantum computers are well suited to rapidly process complex problems that push the limits of conventional computing. This unique ability to perform computations previously deemed intractable will accelerate the creation of new value,  and enable groundbreaking discoveries and problem-solving across numerous fields, including materials science, drug discovery, and financial forecasting.

Fujitsu's quantum computing R & D strategy

Fujitsu, in collaboration with leading research institutes worldwide, promotes R & D across all areas of quantum technology - from hardware (such as quantum devices, integration, and quantum state control) to foundational software (quantum platform and quantum software), as well as practical quantum applications, with the goal of progressing towards widespread commercial use of quantum computers.

World-leading quantum computing technology

Fujitsu is dedicated to leading global R&D in quantum technology, and accelerating its real-world adoption.

World-leading 256-qubit superconducting quantum computer

In April 2025, Fujitsu, in joint development with RIKEN, developed a world-leading 256-qubit superconducting quantum computer. It has been available to companies and research institutions since the first quarter of fiscal 2025.

Press release
Inside Superconducting Quantum Computer.

1,000 qubits computer scheduled for 2026 installation

Fujitsu is currently developing a 1,000-qubit superconducting quantum computer, scheduled to be installed in a new building at the Fujitsu Technology Park and launched in 2026.

Press release

National project for the industrialization of quantum computers

Fujitsu’s medium-term goal is to complete the construction of a superconducting quantum computer with a capacity exceeding 10,000 qubits by fiscal 2030. As part of these efforts, we are participating in a national project promoted by NEDO, which runs until fiscal year 2027.

Press release

Diamond spin quantum computer

Fujitsu is also exploring optional alternatives to superconducting quantum systems. Comparatively, the Diamond spin approach offers superior scalability through optical interconnects. In March 2025, we achieved the world's first high-precision quantum gate operation with an error probability below 0.1%, a crucial step toward large-scale quantum computing.

Press release
Technology news
TECH BLOG

A quantum computing architecture to accelerate the early practical application of quantum computers

The new STAR Architecture, version 3, brings the practical calculation of complex molecular systems much closer to reality.
For calculations involving ruthenium (Ru) catalysts, combining it with molecular optimization dramatically reduces the number of required qubits from approximately 2 million with conventional FTQC to about 20,000, and shortens the estimated computation time from around 5,000 days to roughly 5 days.

Dedicated page

World's largest 40 qubit quantum computer simulator

Since October 2023, Fujitsu has made available, the world's largest 40-qubit quantum computer simulator. This is a significant offering to promote the research and development of practical quantum applications across industries.

Press release

Cloud enabled hybrid quantum platform

Fujitsu provides one of the world's largest combined platforms of quantum computers and quantum simulators. With linkages to external libraries, this allows for seamless switching between noisy real machines and noise-free quantum simulators.

Press release

Open-source software for quantum cloud services

Fujitsu has launched an open-source software initiative, one of the largest of its kind globally, for operating quantum computers in the cloud. It covers everything from setup to operation, and users can customize it freely to meet their individual needs.

Press release
TECH BLOG

Deployments: a solid step toward practical use

[Press Release] Fujitsu to introduce superconducting quantum computer system at National Institute of Advanced Industrial Science and Technology

Please feel free to contact us for more information or for consultation regarding quantum computer implementation, the use of quantum simulators, and joint research. Fujitsu provides comprehensive support for the use of these quantum computers.

Empowering enterprise quantum application development

Fujitsu believes that organizations that will lead the upcoming competitive race for quantum computing will be those who have spent sufficient time researching and developing quantum applications to solve intractable challenges in their respective domains. For this purpose, it is imperative to use quantum simulators in conjunction with quantum computers. Fujitsu offers technologies and solutions that integrate its world-leading superconducting quantum computer, one of the world's largest quantum simulators, as well as proprietary AI technologies through a common platform. This empowers customers to rapidly advance their quantum application development, addressing their specific computational requirements, and solving their domain-specific challenges. Joint research is already underway in Japan, with leading companies across various industries, including materials, medical care, and finance.

Announcing the Fujitsu $100,000 Quantum Simulator Challenge 2025-26

What is "quantum application development" and why should companies start working on it right now?

Solutions from Fujitsu
Fujitsu offers the following solutions:

  • Fujitsu Hybrid Quantum Computing Platform
  • OSS Quantum Cloud Platform Open Quantum Toolchain for Operators & Users

Fujitsu's quantum computer development roadmap

Fujitsu’s ambitious target for fiscal 2030 involves the construction of a large-scale superconducting quantum computer with a capacity exceeding 10,000 qubits. Furthermore, from fiscal 2030 and beyond, we plan to pursue advanced research initiatives targeting the integration of superconducting and diamond spin-based qubits. By fiscal 2035, we aim to realize a 1,000 logical qubit machine, considering the possibility of multiple interconnected quantum bit-chips. Fujitsu is committed to driving forward the long-term development of practical and industrialized quantum computing solutions across all aspects.

Learn more about Fujitsu's quantum computers
